outcome: Add JWT authentication to the Express REST API, including tests.
successCriteria: A JWT-based authentication mechanism is implemented for the Express REST API (token issuance on login plus verification middleware that protects routes)
Protected routes reject requests with a missing, malformed, or expired JWT with 401, and accept requests bearing a valid signed token
The JWT signing secret/key is sourced from configuration/environment (not hardcoded) and tokens carry standard claims (e.g. exp)
Automated tests cover the auth flow: valid-token success, missing/invalid/expired-token rejection, and the login/token-issuance path
The full test suite passes via the project's npm test runner with the new auth tests included

crew.rationale: Locate the existing Express app, route definitions, and any current middleware/auth wiring before changing it.
Final adversarial review of the auth diff.

degradations: No JWT/Express framework detected; docs-research must confirm the auth library.
testRunner unknown; discover the test command.

plan.task: Map the Express app and existing auth.
Implement the JWT middleware and token route until tests pass.
Final code review and run the full npm suite.
